claims.claim: code_ref frontmatter: stt.py:60 documents server-driven turn protocol connected->turn.start->turn.update*->(turn.eager_end->turn.resume?)*->turn.end
claims.found: lines 60-63: 'The server drives the conversation:: connected -> turn.start -> turn.update* -> (turn.eager_end -> turn.resume?)* -> turn.end -> ...'

claims.claim: code_ref frontmatter: stt.py:162 registers the 5 turn event handlers on_turn_start/update/eager_end/resume/end
Transcripts cumulative per turn, no is_final flag and no finalize command (stt.py:65)
claims.found: lines 65-66: 'Transcripts are cumulative per turn; there is no ``is_final`` flag and no ``finalize`` command'

claims.claim: Flux emits four events StartOfTurn/EagerEndOfTurn/TurnResumed/EndOfTurn with the described semantics (deepgram ~542-547)
claims.found: lines 542-547: 'StartOfTurn: user begins speaking, cancel playback / EagerEndOfTurn: medium confidence turn ending, begin speculative reasoning / TurnResumed: user continues, cancel speculative work / EndOfTurn: high confidence completion, finalize response'

claims.claim: Four events appear again in appendix (deepgram ~1989-1994)
claims.found: lines 1989-1994: StartOfTurn / Update / EagerEndOfTurn medium-confidence end (speculative) / TurnResumed / EndOfTurn high-confidence end
line 1986-1987: 'model=flux, language, encoding, sample_rate, eot_threshold, eager_eot_threshold, eot_timeout_ms'. Note cites ~1986 and ~1994; both within the appendix Flux block.

claims.claim: Quote: Flux designed to keep transcripts highly consistent between eager and final boundaries; speculative reasoning rarely diverges from final text (~549)
claims.found: lines 549-551: 'Flux is designed to keep transcripts highly consistent between eager and final turn boundaries. Speculative reasoning rarely diverges from the final text.'

claims.claim: Flux collapses ASR+VAD into one conversational model emitting deterministic turn events (mechanism)
claims.found: lines 539-540: 'Flux simplifies this layer by collapsing traditional ASR and VAD stacks into a single conversational model that emits deterministic turn events'

claims.claim: If adopting Flux/Ink-2-turns must not run own VAD endpointing or desync (single source of truth)
claims.found: lines 558-561: 'downstream VAD and turn logic should be disabled. Redundant detection introduces desynchronization, leading to premature responses... Flux should be the single source of truth for conversational boundaries.'

summary: All 9 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Both frontmatter code_refs (stt.py:60, :162) and the inline stt.py:65 citation match the Cartesia Ink-2 STT clone exactly. All deepgram-ebook source figures (542-547, 1986, 1989-1994, ~549) match the raw parsed PDF verbatim or near-verbatim. The note's elided quote is faithful. PASS.
